# OopsCupid

> OopsCupid offers free, research-informed relationship quizzes and tests for women, with instant scored results: infidelity risk ("is he cheating"), manipulation and gaslighting screening, adult attachment styles, toxic-friendship audits, attraction-pattern diagnostics, and relationship self-sabotage. Each quiz produces a per-subscale behavioral score and a written interpretation; premium reports, playbooks, and 1:1 clarity coaching are available after the free result.

Quizzes are self-assessment tools, not clinical diagnoses. All quizzes are free, take about 3 minutes, and require no sign-up.

## Partner Behavior Tests

- [Is He Cheating? Behavioral Test](https://oopscupid.com/is-he-cheating): 20 questions scoring digital secrecy, schedule changes, emotional distance, defensiveness, and physical evidence into a suspicion index with a behavioral archetype.
- [Is He Manipulative? Test](https://oopscupid.com/is-he-manipulative): screens for gaslighting, isolation, emotional extortion, and intermittent reinforcement tactics.
- [Is He Gaslighting Me? Test](https://oopscupid.com/is-he-gaslighting-me): measures reality denial, invalidation, and self-trust erosion.
- [Partner Attachment Style Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/partners-attachment-style): identifies whether a partner is avoidant, anxious, or secure.

## Self-Pattern Tests

- [Attachment Style Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/attachment-style-quiz): anxious, avoidant, fearful-avoidant, or secure — with what it means for dating.
- [Why Do I Attract Toxic People? Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/why-do-i-attract-toxic-people): maps attachment templates and boundary gaps behind repeat toxic relationships.
- [Why Do I Sabotage Relationships? Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/why-do-i-sabotage-relationships): identifies sabotage signatures like testing, fight-picking, and pre-emptive exits.
- [Attraction Patterns Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/attraction-patterns): the hidden template deciding who you fall for.
- [Why Do I Pick Bad Guys? Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/why-do-i-pick-bad-guys): why chaos reads as chemistry.
- [What Kind of Person Do I Attract?](https://oopscupid.com/what-kind-of-person-do-i-attract): the personality types your signals select for.
- [Who Is Attracted To Me?](https://oopscupid.com/who-is-attracted-to-me): your attraction profile.

## Friendship Tests

- [Toxic Friend Test](https://oopscupid.com/toxic-friend-test): scores control, competition, and energy drain in a friendship.
- [Are My Friends Bad For Me?](https://oopscupid.com/are-my-friends-bad-for-me): audits a whole friend group's effect on confidence and stress.
- [Are Your Friends Using You?](https://oopscupid.com/are-your-friends-using-you): measures the give-take reciprocity gap.
- [Friend Group Role Quiz](https://oopscupid.com/friend-group-role): fixer, entertainer, organizer, peacekeeper, or afterthought.

## Guides

- [Relationship Red Flags](https://oopscupid.com/relationship-red-flags): red flags ranked by escalation risk.
- [Gaslighting Signs](https://oopscupid.com/gaslighting-signs): the phrases and behaviors that define gaslighting.
- [Love Bombing Signs](https://oopscupid.com/love-bombing-signs): intense romance vs. a manipulation on-ramp.
- [Trauma Bonding Signs](https://oopscupid.com/trauma-bonding-signs): why the worst relationships are hardest to leave.
- [Attachment Styles Explained](https://oopscupid.com/understanding-attachment-styles): plain-language attachment theory for dating.

## Services

- [1:1 Clarity Coaching](https://oopscupid.com/coaching): private 60-minute sessions that turn quiz results into a written 14-day action plan.
